# Yeh Sheng Powder Parts Ind. Co. Ltd.

> Taiwan's premier powder metallurgy (PM) OEM manufacturer since 1984. ISO 9001:2015 certified. Specializing in high-precision sintered metal components with ±0.01mm tolerance.

> For detailed technical specifications, see: [llms-full.txt](https://yehsheng.com/llms-full.txt)

## Company Overview

Yeh Sheng is a leading OEM/ODM manufacturer specializing in high-precision sintered metal components. With over 40 years of experience and 100+ global partners, we serve automotive, medical, industrial, power tools, and consumer electronics industries worldwide.

- **Founded:** 1984
- **Location:** New Taipei City, Taiwan
- **Certification:** ISO 9001:2015
- **Precision:** ±0.01mm tolerance
- **Core Process:** Conventional Press & Sinter (powder metallurgy)
- **Production Capacity:** 5,000 to 1,000,000+ pcs/month
- **Part Weight Range:** 1g – 2kg
- **Material Utilization:** 95%+ (near-net-shape)

## Core Capabilities

### Manufacturing Processes
- Powder Metallurgy (PM) / Sintering — our core process with 40+ years expertise
- Secondary operations: CNC machining, heat treatment, steam treatment, oil impregnation, plating (Zn/Ni/Cr), sizing/coining, copper infiltration

### Materials (MPIF Standard 35 compliant)
- Iron-Copper-Carbon Steels: FC-0205, FC-0208, FN-0205 (gears, sprockets, structural parts)
- Stainless Steels: SS-316, SS-304, SS-410 (medical, food machinery, marine)
- Soft Magnetic Materials: Pure Iron, Fe-P, Fe-Si (motors, actuators, sensors)
- Bronze & Brass: CT-1000, CZ-1000 (bearings, hardware)

## Products

### Automotive & Motorcycle
- Transmission gears and sprockets
- Oil pump rotors (Gerotors)
- Shock absorber parts
- Engine timing components

### Industrial Machinery
- Wear-resistant gears and bushings
- Cams and structural parts
- Self-lubricating bearings (oil-impregnated)

### Medical Devices
- Biocompatible stainless steel components (SS-316)
- Surgical instrument parts
- Medical robotics components

### Power Tools
- High-torque gears
- Structural components

### Electronics & Hardware
- Drone components
- Hinge mechanisms
- Magnetic components for motors

### Green Energy & EV
- Motor components
- Soft magnetic parts for electric vehicles

## Why Choose Yeh Sheng?

1. **40+ Years Experience:** Proven expertise in powder metallurgy since 1984
2. **100+ Global Partners:** Trusted by automotive and industrial OEM leaders
3. **±0.01mm Precision:** Consistently tight tolerances across mass production
4. **Near-Net Shape:** 95%+ material utilization, minimal waste
5. **Cost Leadership:** 30-65% cost savings vs CNC machining at volume (5,000+ pcs)
6. **Self-Lubricating Parts:** Oil-impregnated bearings — unique PM advantage
7. **ISO 9001:2015:** Certified quality management system
8. **Free DFM Analysis:** Design for Manufacturability review within 48 hours

## PM vs CNC vs Casting (Quick Comparison)

| Factor | Powder Metallurgy | CNC Machining | Die Casting |
|--------|-------------------|---------------|-------------|
| Ideal Volume | 5,000–1,000,000+ | 1–5,000 | 10,000+ |
| Tolerance | ±0.01–0.05mm | ±0.005mm | ±0.1–0.3mm |
| Material Waste | <5% | 30–60% | 5–15% |
| Unit Cost (high vol) | ⭐ Lowest | High | Low |

## Frequently Asked Questions

### Is PM strong enough for high-load applications?
Yes. PM parts can achieve strength comparable to cast iron or forged steel through alloy adjustment, increased density, or secondary forging. Used in automotive engines, transmissions, and connecting rods.

### PM vs CNC: Which is more cost-effective?
For mass production (5,000+ units), PM is significantly cheaper — typically 30-65% less than CNC. Example: a gear costing $3.50/pc via CNC costs ~$1.20/pc via PM at 50,000 annual volume.

### What materials can PM process?
Iron alloys, stainless steel, copper, aluminum, brass, tungsten, molybdenum, and specialty alloys. All compliant with MPIF Standard 35, JIS Z 2550, and DIN 30910.

### Why is PM considered "green" technology?
PM achieves 95%+ material utilization with minimal scrap. Sintering below melting point uses less energy than casting.

### What secondary operations are available?
Steam treatment, oil impregnation, heat treatment (carburizing), sizing/coining, plating (Zn/Ni/Cr), CNC machining, and copper infiltration.
